slides.title: Animal Groups
slides.content:
There are many groups of animals. Let's learn the main ones!

🐕 Mammals have fur or hair on their skin. Dogs, cats and elephants are mammals.

🐦 Birds have feathers and wings. Most birds can fly!

🐟 Fish have scales and gills to breathe underwater.

🐸 Amphibians can live in water and on land. Frogs are amphibians.

slides.title: How Are Babies Born?
slides.content:
Some animals lay eggs. Birds, fish, reptiles and amphibians all lay eggs.

Most mammals do NOT lay eggs. They have live babies. A cat has kittens, a dog has puppies!

Baby frogs are called tadpoles. They live in water. When they grow up, they can jump on land!
slides.tip: Almost all animals that are NOT mammals lay eggs!

slides.title: Subject Pronouns
slides.content:
Subject pronouns replace the name of a person or thing:

👤 I — the person speaking
👤 You — the person listening
👦 He — a boy or man
👧 She — a girl or woman
🐕 It — an animal or thing
👥 We — the speaker + others
👥 They — other people, animals or things
slides.tip: Use 'it' for one animal or thing. Use 'they' for more than one!

slides.title: Using Subject Pronouns
slides.problem:
Replace the underlined words with a subject pronoun:

1. The elephant is very big.
2. Sara and Tom like animals.
3. My mum loves cats.
steps: 1️⃣ The elephant is one animal → use 'it'
2️⃣ Sara and Tom are more than one person → use 'they'
3️⃣ My mum is one woman → use 'she'
slides.answer: 1. It is very big. 2. They like animals. 3. She loves cats.

slides.title: A Day at the Zoo
slides.html:
Tom and his family went to the zoo. They saw many amazing animals!

First, they saw the lions. 'It has beautiful fur!' said Tom about a big lion.

Then they visited the birds. They had colourful feathers and long wings.

Next, he looked at the fish. The fish had shiny scales and used their gills to breathe.

'I love the zoo!' said Tom. 'We should come again!'

slides.title: Animal Body Coverings
slides.content:
Different animals have different things on their skin:

🐕 Fur — soft hair that keeps mammals warm. Dogs and cats have fur.

🐦 Feathers — light coverings that help birds fly. They also keep birds warm.

🐟 Scales — hard, flat pieces on fish and reptiles. They protect the animal.

🐸 Amphibians have smooth, wet skin with no fur, feathers or scales!

slides.title: Compound Nouns
slides.content:
A compound noun is made of two words put together to make a new word:

🐟 goldfish = gold + fish
🦋 butterfly = butter + fly
🐦 birdhouse = bird + house
🐈 catfish = cat + fish

The two small words give you a clue about the meaning!
slides.tip: A goldfish is not made of gold, and a butterfly is not made of butter! But the words help you remember.

slides.title: Make Compound Nouns
slides.problem:
Match the words to make compound nouns:

1. rain + ?
2. sun + ?
3. star + ?
Words: fish, bow, flower
steps: 1️⃣ rain + bow → rainbow (the colourful arc in the sky)
2️⃣ sun + flower → sunflower (a big yellow flower)
3️⃣ star + fish → starfish (a sea animal shaped like a star)

slides.title: The Life of a Frog
slides.html:
A frog is an amphibian. It has a very interesting life!

First, the mother frog lays eggs in the water. The eggs are very small and soft.

Then, baby frogs come out of the eggs. They are called tadpoles. They live in water and breathe with gills, just like fish!

Slowly, the tadpoles grow legs. They lose their tails. Now they are frogs!

Adult frogs can live on land and in water. They have smooth, wet skin and strong legs to jump.

slides.title: He, She or It?
slides.problem:
Choose the correct pronoun: he, she or it?

1. My dad is tall. ___ plays football.
2. The cat is sleeping. ___ is tired.
3. My sister likes music. ___ sings well.
slides.answer: 1. He plays football. 2. It is tired. 3. She sings well.

slides.title: Let's Review!
points: 🐾 Mammals have fur, birds have feathers, fish have scales
🐟 Fish breathe with gills; amphibians live in water AND on land
🥚 Birds, fish, reptiles and amphibians lay eggs; most mammals do not
👤 Subject pronouns: I, you, he, she, it, we, they
🔗 Compound nouns: two words together make a new word (goldfish, butterfly, rainbow)
